name: Anne Frank
title: A Brief Life of Anne Frank
story: Annelies Marie Frank was born on 12 June 1929 in Frankfurt to Otto and Edith Frank. She grew up with her older sister Margot in a liberal Jewish household. When the National Socialists rose to power, the family saw no secure future in Germany and moved to Amsterdam in 1934.

In the Netherlands Anne attended school, made friends, and learned Dutch. After Germany invaded in 1940, anti-Jewish decrees stripped the Franks of work, travel, and public life. In July 1942, when Margot was ordered to report for deportation, the family went into hiding in the secret annex behind Otto Frank's business premises at Prinsengracht 263.

For more than two years Anne lived in confinement with seven others. She read, studied, quarreled and reconciled with the adults, and poured her hopes, fears, and sharp observations into a diary she had received for her thirteenth birthday. She imagined it might one day become a book about life in hiding.

On 4 August 1944 the annex was betrayed and raided. The occupants were arrested and sent through Westerbork to Auschwitz-Birkenau. Anne and Margot were later transferred to Bergen-Belsen. Both sisters died of typhus in February or March 1945, only weeks before the camp was liberated.

Otto Frank, the sole survivor of the family, returned to Amsterdam. Miep Gies had saved Anne's papers. They became The Diary of a Young Girl—one of the most read testimonies of the Shoah, translated into dozens of languages and read by generations as a voice of conscience, courage, and the demand never to forget.
